Source of data: Abstract: "Settlement was significantly lower in the sediment treatments where it reached only 71% of levels in reference areas." Evidence also aligns with Fig/Table statement: "Settlement in the sediment treatment areas was 71% of that in the reference areas (Table 2, χ[2 ] = 5.32, p=0.021)."

Abstract/Results: "number of juvenile corals surviving at sediment treated sites after 8 months was 39% of levels at reference sites" (Abstract) and "by this time they were 39% of the number surviving in the reference sites" (Results). Mortality fraction relative to reference survival is therefore 1-0.39 = 0.61; no direct mortality proportion is explicitly reported.

Abstract/Results define reference as the 100% level for comparison: "Settlement was significantly lower in the sediment treatments where it reached only 71% of levels in reference areas." Therefore reference settlement corresponds to 1.0 by definition; absolute settlement counts are not provided in the text.

Unit of measurement: fraction (1 - survival fraction) relative to reference
Source of data: Abstract/Results: survival in sediment treatments is 39% of reference after 8 months. By definition, reference survival is 100% relative to itself; this implies 0 relative mortality. Absolute mortality/survival fractions are not reported for reference in the text.
